methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.8B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

private.name: Saronic
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $9.25B

data.narrative:
Saildrone is a Series D defense and security company that has raised $300M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (trading under DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage, including Saronic, Chaos Industries, Epirus, Anduril Industries, and Flock Safety.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $1.8B and applying a typical Series D step-up multiplier of 1.5x, implies a valuation of $2.6B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Saronic ($9.2B), Chaos Industries ($4.5B), and Epirus ($1.4B)—yields a median valuation of $4.5B, with a range spanning $1.4B to $9.2B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.2B to $5.2B, with a median of $3.6B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables establish the upper bound, reflecting variance in scale and maturity within the Defense & Security cohort. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, contract duration and renewal risk, and the relative stage advancement of peer companies within Series D.
